content.Text: [Sun 2026-04-19 00:28 PDT] Build a new interactive HTML tool: **Maximum Matching Finder** for the GraphVisual project. Create the file `docs/matching.html` — a self-contained interactive tool for finding maximum matchings in graphs. ## Design Requirements Follow the exact same visual style as existing tools (see `docs/vertex-cover.html`, `docs/dominating-set.html` for reference). Use the shared `styles.css` stylesheet. Dark theme with CSS variables (--surface, --border, --accent, --text, --bg, etc.). ## Layout - Left panel (320px): controls - Right: Canvas for graph visualization - Stats bar below canvas - Step history panel ## Features ### Algorithms (3): 1. **Greedy Matching** — simple greedy heuristic 2. **Augmenting Path (Berge)** — find augmenting paths to improve matching 3. **Hopcroft-Karp** — efficient bipartite matching (only enabled for bipartite graphs) ### Interactive Canvas: - Click to add nodes, click two nodes to add edge - Drag nodes to reposition - Delete nodes/edges with right-click - Matched edges shown in bold accent color, unmatched edges in default - Free (unmatched) vertices shown with distinct color - Augmenting path highlighted during animation ### 8 Preset Graphs: 1. Triangle (K3) 2. Complete Bipartite K3,3 3. Petersen Graph 4. Path P6 5. Cycle C6 6. Star K1,5 7. Grid 3×3 8. Random (10 nodes, ~15 edges) ### Animation: - Step-by-step execution with play/pause/step controls - Speed slider (100ms to 2000ms) - Step history list showing each augmenting path found ### Stats: - Matching size / max possible - Free vertices count - Is perfect matching? - Algorithm used ### Text Input: - Textarea for edge list input (one edge per line: "0 1") - Parse button ## Sidebar Navigation Add the matching tool to the sidebar nav. Copy the exact sidebar HTML from vertex-cover.html but add a new entry: ```html 💍Max Matching ``` Place it after the vertex-cover link in the Tools section. Also add links for euler.html, toposort.html, hamiltonian.html, scc.html, vertex-cover.html, resilience.html if they're missing from the sidebar. Make sure the sidebar in matching.html includes ALL existing tool links from index.html's sidebar, plus the new matching entry, plus vertex-cover.html and other tools that might have been added after index.html. ## Technical Notes - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no external dependencies - All graph algorithms implemented in JavaScript within the file - Canvas 2D rendering (not SVG) - The file should be ~800-1200 lines, fully functional ## Also update `docs/index.html`: Add a sidebar link for matching.html in the Tools section: ```html 💍Max Matching ``` After creating, verify the HTML is valid by checking the file exists and has proper closing tags.
I’m inspecting the existing `docs` tools and sidebar structure first so the new page matches the project’s established layout and styling before I add `matching.html` and update `index.html`.

I’ve confirmed the newer tool pages already diverged from `index.html`, so I’m collecting the superset of sidebar links before I build the matching page. After that I’ll add the new tool and patch `index.html` consistently.

The docs set is inconsistent: `index.html` is missing some newer tools, while other pages introduced a separate “Analysis” section. I’m checking what files actually exist so the new sidebar includes a defensible superset instead of guessing.

ToolUse.name: Run Get-ChildItem -Path docs -Filter *.html | Select-String -Pattern 'hamiltonian|scc|vertex-cover|resilience|euler|toposort|matching\.html' | ForEach-Object { "{0}:{1}" -f $_.Path.Replace((Get-Location).Path + '\\',''), $_.Line.Trim() }
ToolUse.raw_input: {"call_id":"call_y5WVZlHOUQ3TAUmIXx9MieZz","turn_id":"019da4a4-7f2e-7b52-a93c-f77a92e17416","command":["C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe","-Command","Get-ChildItem -Path docs -Filter *.html | Select-String -Pattern 'hamiltonian|scc|vertex-cover|resilience|euler|toposort|matching\\.html' | ForEach-Object { \"{0}:{1}\" -f $_.Path.Replace((Get-Location).Path + '\\\\',''), $_.Line.Trim() }"],"cwd":"C:\\Users\\onlin\\.openclaw\\workspace\\temp-builder","parsed_cmd":[{"type":"unknown","cmd":"Get-ChildItem -Path docs -Filter *.html | Select-String -Pattern 'hamiltonian|scc|vertex-cover|resilience|euler|toposort|matching\\.html' | ForEach-Object { \"{0}:{1}\" -f $_.Path.Replace((Get-Location).Path + '\\\\',''), $_.Line.Trim() }"}],"source":"agent"}

👑 Dominating Set Finder Find minimum dominating sets with greedy & exact algorithms

A dominating set S of a graph G is a subset of vertices such that every vertex is either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Finding a minimum dominating set is NP-hard, but greedy approximation works well in practice. This tool lets you visualize both approaches step by step.

About Dominating Sets

📡

Wireless Networks

Dominating sets model optimal placement of relay nodes — every device is within one hop of a relay.

🏥

Facility Location

Place facilities (hospitals, fire stations) so every community is adjacent to at least one.

🔒

Network Monitoring

Position sensors to observe all network links — a dominating set monitors every neighbor.

🧬

Biological Networks

Identify essential proteins in PPI networks: a dominating set covers all interaction partners.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

🛡️ Minimum Vertex Cover Finder Find minimum vertex covers with greedy, exact & König algorithms

A vertex cover of a graph G is a set S of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in S. Finding a minimum vertex cover is NP-hard, but a simple greedy 2-approximation exists. For bipartite graphs, König's theorem gives an exact solution in polynomial time via maximum matching.

Links
💻GitHub 📦Releases

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
🏗️

Architecture

Project structure, data pipeline, algorithm details, component design.
🗄️

Database Schema

PostgreSQL table schemas, query patterns, data pipeline ordering.
📖

API Reference

Detailed class and method documentation for all public APIs.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT
